prompt: Read specs/test-analysis.md. A test is failing. Diagnose whether the Generator wrote a bad test (wrong selector) or it is a real defect. The heading on the page is a plain <h1>. If the test uses a wrong selector, FIX the selector in projects/healer-test/tests/homepage.spec.ts and re-run: TEST_PROJECT=healer-test npx playwright test homepage.spec.ts. Do NOT log a test bug to docs/issues.csv. Report what you fixed.
output:
I'll start by reading the relevant files to understand the project and the failing test. The analysis is clear: TC-002 uses `#main-title` but the page has a plain `

` with no `id`. This is a **test-side selector bug**. Fixing it now. Both tests pass. Now update `specs/test-analysis.md` to record the heal: --- ## Healer Report ### What was wrong — TC-002: *heading is present* **Classification: Test-case bug (wrong selector)** — not an application defect. The Generator wrote: ```typescript await expect(page.locator('#main-title')).toHaveText('Example Domain'); ``` The live page (`https://example.com`) has `

Example Domain

` — a plain `

` **with no `id` attribute**. The selector `#main-title` matches nothing, causing a timeout. ### Fix applied — `projects/healer-test/tests/homepage.spec.ts` ```diff - await expect(page.locator('#main-title')).toHaveText('Example Domain'); + await expect(page.locator('h1')).toHaveText('Example Domain'); ``` This matches the already-confirmed selector used in TC-001 on the same page. ### Post-fix result | Test | Status | |---|---| | TC-001: homepage heading text | ✅ PASS | | TC-002: heading is present | ✅ PASS | **Pass rate: 2/2 (100%)** No entries added to `docs/issues.csv` — this was a test-side bug only. --- ### Hand-off to `playwright-test-executor-analyst` Healing is complete. Please re-run the full suite and produce the final report/verdict. Changed files: - `projects/healer-test/tests/homepage.spec.ts` — selector fix in TC-002 - `projects/healer-test/specs/test-analysis.md` — updated with healer fix record No open application defects remain.
